title: podman 4 internal DNS is case sensitive
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

When using a custom bridge network with the DNS plugin enabled, resolving container names is case sensitive. DNS look-ups are not expected to treat case significantly, so this breaks any software relying on that fact (for example, OpenSSH tends to make hostnames lowercase before sending them to the resolver, leading to container hostnames with uppercase letters being resolved incorrectly).

This is a change in behaviour from podman 3.x.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `podman network create blah`

2. `podman run --rm --network blah --name TestCase -it alpine`

3. Resolve the name as `TestCase` and `testcase`, and observe the difference

**Describe the results you received:**

When resolving exactly the container's hostname, I receive the correct answer. When changing the hostname to a different casing, 0.0.0.0 is returned (which is generally treated as IPv4 localhost in a destination address context).

```
/ # cat /etc/resolv.conf
search dns.podman lan
nameserver 10.89.0.1
nameserver 192.168.127.1
/ # ping -c 1 TestCase
PING TestCase (10.89.0.2): 56 data bytes
64 bytes from 10.89.0.2: seq=0 ttl=42 time=0.126 ms

--- TestCase ping statistics ---
1 packets transmitted, 1 packets received, 0% packet loss
round-trip min/avg/max = 0.126/0.126/0.126 ms
/ # ping -c 1 testcase
PING testcase (0.0.0.0): 56 data bytes
64 bytes from 127.0.0.1: seq=0 ttl=42 time=0.049 ms

--- testcase ping statistics ---
1 packets transmitted, 1 packets received, 0% packet loss
round-trip min/avg/max = 0.049/0.049/0.049 ms
/ # ping -c 1 TESTCASE
PING TESTCASE (0.0.0.0): 56 data bytes
64 bytes from 127.0.0.1: seq=0 ttl=42 time=0.073 ms

--- TESTCASE ping statistics ---
1 packets transmitted, 1 packets received, 0% packet loss
round-trip min/avg/max = 0.073/0.073/0.073 ms
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

DNS look-ups should not be case-sensitive, so I would expect internal container hostname resolution to obey the same rules as hostnames resolved via host DNS. E.g.,:

```
/ # ping -c 1 github.com
PING github.com (140.82.121.4): 56 data bytes
64 bytes from 140.82.121.4: seq=0 ttl=42 time=4.392 ms

--- github.com ping statistics ---
1 packets transmitted, 1 packets received, 0% packet loss
round-trip min/avg/max = 4.392/4.392/4.392 ms
/ # ping -c 1 GITHUB.COM
PING GITHUB.COM (140.82.121.4): 56 data bytes
64 bytes from 140.82.121.4: seq=0 ttl=42 time=1.455 ms

--- GITHUB.COM ping statistics ---
1 packets transmitted, 1 packets received, 0% packet loss
round-trip min/avg/max = 1.455/1.455/1.455 ms
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

N/A

**Output of `podman version`:**

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

MacOS 12.4, ARM64/Apple M1 Pro

comments.body: Hi @theburge , Thanks for creating the issue. Since you are using netavark we can fix this at aardvark end. Following PR should close this once tested and merged https://github.com/containers/aardvark-dns/pull/146
This will be fixed in next release.
